source_jobs.title: Principal Enterprise Architect
source_jobs.description:
TOMS is in business to improve lives. When you wear or work with TOMS, you help support children’s education, health and well-being, giving kids everywhere the gift of better tomorrows.

About the Role:

TOMS is seeking a Principal Enterprise Architect to join our AI & Enterprise Technology team. As a senior IC and technical lead, you will set architectural direction for a blended team of engineers and contractors, evolve our enterprise platform, and drive AI, integration, and cloud initiatives that directly support TOMS business goals. You will partner with executive leadership and cross-functional stakeholders to align technology investments with business outcomes while elevating the technical capabilities of the teams around you.

The Impact You’ll Make:

Technical Leadership

 - Serve as the day-to-day technical lead for a blended team of junior engineers and contractors — guiding designs, unblocking delivery, and elevating quality through hands-on mentorship and code reviews.

 - Act as the primary technical escalation point, helping the team navigate complex trade-offs, resolve ambiguity, and make sound architectural decisions under pressure.

 - Break down complex initiatives into clear, actionable work and invest in the long-term growth of junior engineers through structured walkthroughs and stretch assignments.

Platform & Systems Design

 - Lead and drive the enterprise architecture roadmap, establishing patterns, standards, and guardrails across product, data, and infrastructure domains.

 - Lead architecture reviews for major initiatives, ensuring solutions are scalable, secure, and aligned with long-term business goals.

 - Drive the evolution of Tom's core platforms — commerce, supply chain, and customer experience — and establish reusable reference architectures that accelerate team velocity.

Integration Architecture

 - Lead the definition of Tom's integration strategy — setting standards for how internal systems, third-party platforms, and external partners connect via APIs, events, and messaging.

 - Lead the design and governance of API and event-driven integration patterns, including REST, GraphQL, and streaming architectures, ensuring reliability, scalability, and security across the enterprise.

 - Lead the evaluation and adoption of integration platforms (e.g., MuleSoft, Boomi, Kafka, EventBridge), providing guidance on fit, trade-offs, and total cost of ownership.

 - Establish data exchange standards and integration contracts between core systems — ERP, commerce, supply chain, and customer data — and ensure integrations are well-documented, testable, and built for long-term maintainability.

AI Enablement & Innovation

 - Lead the identification and architecture of high-impact AI/ML opportunities across commerce, supply chain, and customer experience — including the foundational infrastructure for model serving, feature stores, and experimentation frameworks.

 - Lead the evaluation of AI tools, platforms, and vendors — including generative AI solutions — providing clear guidance on build vs. buy trade-offs, integration risk, and long-term scalability.

 - Establish governance frameworks for responsible AI adoption, covering data privacy, model transparency, and bias mitigation standards.

 - Partner closely with product and business teams to translate AI opportunities into concrete architectural requirements and delivery roadmaps, and stay current on emerging trends to inform leadership decisions.

Customer & Partner Facing

 - Partner with product, commercial, and operations leaders to translate business strategy into actionable technology architecture.

 - Represent Tom's technical capabilities in vendor negotiations and partner integrations; communicate architectural decisions and trade-offs clearly to executive stakeholders.

What You Bring:

 - 12+ years in technology with 5+ years in enterprise or solutions architecture at a principal or staff level.

 - Proven experience as a technical lead fo

source_jobs.title: Digital Marketing & eCommerce Intern - EMEA
source_jobs.description:
About TOMS

TOMS is in business to improve lives. When you wear or work with TOMS, you help positively impact lives — 106 million and counting. Every TOMS purchase helps support children’s education, health, and well-being, giving kids everywhere the gift of a better tomorrow.

As a Certified B Corp™, we are proud to meet high standards of transparency, accountability, and social and environmental performance.



About The Role

The Digital Marketing & eCommerce Intern will work closely with the eCommerce team to support email marketing, digital campaigns, and internal content creation. This role is ideal for someone who is passionate about eCommerce, social media trends, and digital storytelling, with a strong interest in platforms such as TikTok, Instagram, and Google.

The ideal candidate is highly organized, proactive, and enthusiastic about the fashion industry, with a strong interest in fashion and digital culture.



What You’ll Do

· Support the planning and execution of digital marketing campaigns across email, social media, and eCommerce touchpoints.

· Assist in creating clear and engaging creative briefs for campaign assets, newsletters, and social activations.

· Stay up to date on TikTok, Instagram, and fashion trends, identifying opportunities for TOMS to create relevant and engaging content across digital channels.

· Research trending formats, sounds, and cultural moments to inspire campaign ideas and content creation.

· Assist in the creation and execution of newsletter campaigns.

· Monitor and report on campaign, email, and social media performance, sharing insights and recommendations.

· Support day-to-day eCommerce operations and administrative tasks as needed.



What You’ll Bring

· Strong organizational skills and attention to detail, particularly when managing briefs and coordinating campaigns.

· Interest in email marketing, eCommerce, and digital campaigns within the fashion industry.

· Passion for digital marketing and social media, especially Instagram and TikTok, with a strong understanding of trends and platform culture.

· Eagerness to learn new skills and work with email marketing and eCommerce tools such as Attentive, Shopify and analytics platforms.

· A proactive mindset with a “can-do” attitude, bringing fresh ideas and an analytical approach to the team.

· Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English; French, German, or Dutch are considered a plus.



Requirements

· Based in Amsterdam or able to commute to Amsterdam two days per week (no relocation provided).

· Available starting August 2026.

· Must be enrolled, for the duration of the internship, in a Marketing, Communications, Media, or Business-related program at an accredited college or university (Bachelor’s or Master’s degree).



What We Give

· A 5- or 6-month internship (32 to 40 hours per week).

· Start date: August 2026.

· Two vacation days per month based on a 40-hour work week.

· Two free pairs of TOMS shoes.

· Hybrid working model: two days per week at the EMEA office after onboarding.

· Internship allowance aligned with market conditions.



EEO

TOMS is an Equal Employment Opportunity, committed to intentional anti-racism in all areas and will consider all qualified applicants. Our diverse backgrounds, perspectives, identities, and talents help us thrive.

source_jobs.title: Director, Digital Experience & Conversion
source_jobs.description:
TOMS is in business to improve lives. When you wear or work with TOMS, you help support children’s education, health and well-being, giving kids everywhere the gift of better tomorrows.

About the Role:

The Director, Digital Experience & Conversion is responsible for defining and driving the end-to-end customer experience across TOMS’ digital platforms, with a focus on conversion, usability, and brand consistency. This role leads UX strategy, design, and optimization efforts across eCommerce and digital touchpoints, partnering closely with Engineering and business stakeholders to deliver high-impact customer experiences.

The Impact You’ll Make:

 - Own and drive the end-to-end user experience strategy across TOMS’ digital platforms, focusing on customer journey quality, conversion rate, usability, and engagement across key touchpoints

 - Lead UX design and execution for eCommerce experiences, including navigation, product discovery, PDP, cart, checkout, and other high-value customer interactions.

 - Own conversion rate and digital funnel performance across key eCommerce journeys, leveraging data, testing, and insights to continuously improve outcomes.

 - Partner with business stakeholders to translate requirements into intuitive, scalable, and high-performing user experiences that balance brand, functionality, and speed.

 - Drive continuous optimization through testing, analytics, and user insights, improving conversion, engagement, and overall digital performance.

 - Establish and maintain UX standards, design systems, interaction patterns, and best practices to ensure consistency, accessibility, and quality across all digital experiences

 - Build and evolve UX capabilities, processes, and working norms, operating with a hands-on, problem-solving mindset in a lean environment to support execution and ensure high-quality outcomes.

What You Bring:

 - 8+ years of experience in UX, product design, digital experience, or related disciplines.

 - Proven experience leading UX strategy and design execution across eCommerce or customer-facing digital platforms.

 - Experience with UX design leadership, digital customer journey design, and usability optimization

 - eCommerce experience design and conversion optimization

 - Solid understanding of design systems, interaction standards, and accessibility best practices

 - Testing, analytics, and user-insight-driven optimization

 - Cross-functional collaboration with Engineering, Marketing, and business stakeholders

 - Ability to lead through influence and elevate quality across the organization

 - Strong portfolio demonstrating ownership of customer journey, conversion-focused experiences, usability improvements, and digital design systems.

 - Experience partnering closely with Engineering, Product, Marketing, and business stakeholders to bring digital experiences from concept through launch.

 - Demonstrated ability to use analytics, testing, and user insights to improve customer experience and business outcomes.

 - Experience with Shopify or similar eCommerce platforms.

 - Retail, consumer, fashion, or direct-to-consumer brand experience is preferred.

 - Experience with A/B testing, CRO, design systems, and user research methodologies is preferred.

 - Bachelor’s degree in Design, Human-Computer Interaction, UX, Psychology, Marketing, or related field, or equivalent practical experience is preferred.

 - A genuine enthusiasm for TOMS' mission and a commitment to making kindness in business the norm.

What We Give:

 - This is a Direct Hire role with a target annual salary range of $180,000 - $190,000 based on experience, plus an annual bonus based on the company's performance

 - Hybrid/Remote work environment - whatever works best for you and your team

 - A $600 stipend when you join to furnish your home office how you like, plus $100 annually to refresh your supplies and gear

 - Medical, dental, and vision insurance; 401K with company matching

 - A generous PTO

source_jobs.title: Sales Manager - UK
source_jobs.description:
About TOMS

TOMS is in business to improve lives. When you wear or work with TOMS, you help support children’s education, health and well-being, giving kids everywhere the gift of better tomorrows.

 

About the Role

The ideal candidate will be responsible for building and delivering the UK sales strategy to drive our growth ambition through a holistic approach, sell in and sell through and increased profitability in order to achieve an optimal result and according to budget.

Key Responsibilities

 - Create and deliver UK Sales strategy to build the TOMS brand for the future, aligned with broader EMEA strategy

 - Lead the UK Sales annual plan, through translation and deployment of the EMEA strategic direction to maximize the potential in market.

 - Lead Sales team to deliver annual budget through strategic direction and delivery of comprehensive Sales plan.

 - Drive the UK implementation of EMEA strategic goals, delivery of Focus lines and create UK specific product segmentation strategy.

 - Lead the country Sales team, through clear communications of targets and objectives.

 - Attract, develop and retain the right caliber of talent in the market.

 - Support, develop and lead the team to maximise UK potential. Monitor and support individual performance of team members and deliver regular coaching and feedback to ensure growth and key learnings are achieved in their positions

 - Improve Wholesale performance by analysis of (proven) sales drivers and setting stretching targets.

 - Develop a strong approach to leading and managing the growth of UK Accounts, as well as accurate product and Sales forecasting.

 - Identify new business opportunities with new or existing customers through solid understanding of business risks and opportunities.

 - Manage strategic Key Accounts where necessary.

 - Deliver exceptional preparation and GTM strategies for selling seasons.

 - Delivery of Monthly/ Quarterly shipping budgets.

 - Work closely with Marketing team to ensure Country allignment and support for locally relevant programs to boost sales and develop/renew customer relations in the market.

 - Deliver Quarterly UK business review.

 - Responsible for management of the UK Office/ Showroom.

 - Attendance at Sales launches and relevant trade shows.

 

What You Bring

 - Provide inspirational leadership and direction to direct report team and ensure the continued development and management of a professional and efficient organization.

 - Cultivate strong and transparent working relationships across the organization, to ensure delivery of order book, relevant product opportunities and exposure in market (Credit, Customer Service, Marketing and Product)

 - Have exceptional understanding of the UK market and customer base across all customer channels, as well as market trends

 - Experience of branded Wholesale sales in fashion and specific experience in the footwear business

 - Ability to build a comprehensive strategic plan

 - Ability to understand and audit competitive landscapes in the market.

 - A self-starter, someone versatile and eager to identify unique opportunities of growth and market penetration

 - Establishes strong relationships with accounts to enable team support

 - Strong ability to work as a team player and managerial experience to lead, develop and engage the team.

 - Strategic thinker with excellent analytical and project management skills

 - Ability to use sound judgment in decision making with results-driven orientation, work under pressure and meet deadlines

 - Clear and strong communicator, both verbally and in writing

 - Good negotiating and sales skills

 - Exceptional planning and organizational skills

Qualifications

 - Minimum of 5-7 years’ management experience in a Commercial role.

 - Willingness to learn and develop in a fast paced pan-continental environment.

 - Intermediate knowledge of Microsoft Office (Power Point, Word and Excel).

 - Have exceptional understanding of the UK

notes: TOMS is a public, socially responsible company with a small job board and a mix of roles.
